title: ADR-046b federation OIDC token exchange reference architecture (v21 cycle-11 P0)
author: KooshaPari
device_rationale: Pure doc authoring (Markdown + gh api); zero cargo / dind / sim / heavy cargo test --workspace
device_gate_self_check: Rule 1 PASS / Rule 2 N/A / Rule 3 PASS
adr_anchors: docs/adr/2026-06-22/ADR-046c-federation-p2p.md (forthcoming per ADR-046c index cross-ref, authored by parallel subagent)

sections_authored.context: 3 concrete use cases (cross-org delegation, service impersonation, step-up authentication) and the relationship to ADR-046 (mTLS transport) / ADR-079 (inbound JWT verification).
sections_authored.decision: 8 sub-decisions: RFC 8693 token exchange protocol adoption (delegation/impersonation/step-up modes sanctioned), IdP trust establishment (two-phase governance-gated handshake), audience/scope claims enforcement (aud/scope/azp/act), refresh token rotation (single-use, sender-constrained, 30-day max), JWKS key discovery (5-min TTL + 10% jitter, kid-miss retry once), clock-skew tolerance (60s symmetric), revocation (RFC 7009 online + RFC 7662 introspection, 500ms budget, 5-min propagation), standards references.
sections_authored.consequences: Positive (auditable delegation, governed IdP onboarding, mandatory rotation, bounded revocation, standards-aligned) + Negative (onboarding friction, IdP support requirements, NTP dependency, introspection latency) + Neutral (inbound unchanged, mTLS unchanged).
sections_authored.alternatives: 5 rejected: forward-original-JWT (loses delegation chain), SAML 2.0 exchange (legacy XML, no token_type), custom JWT exchange (reinvents RFC 8693), draft-ietf-oauth-mutual (deprecated 2024, redundant with mTLS+token-binding), status quo (cross-org API-key sprawl).
sections_authored.appendix_a_companions: ADR-046 (mTLS transport substrate, ACCEPTED); ADR-046c (peer-to-peer federation topology, FORTHCOMING — see docs/adr/2026-06-22/ADR-046c-federation-p2p.md cross-ref index; in-flight at write time on parallel subagent branch).
sections_authored.appendix_b_cross_references: 12 references: ADR-046 (mTLS transport), ADR-079 (inbound OIDC verification), ADR-077 (Vault migration roadmap), ADR-040 (test coverage gates), ADR-042 (security audit cadence), ADR-049 (app-substrate drift detector), ADR-051 (Bifrost-as-library), ADR-012 / ADR-036B (pheno-tracing), 71-pillar framework (ADR-024), RFC 8693/7009/7662/7519/8705/9449 + OIDC Core 1.0 + OIDC Discovery 1.0 + OAuth Security BCP §4.13 + OWASP ASVS v4.0.3 V3.5/V3.7/V6.4 + NIST SP 800-63B §7.1.

standards_adopted: RFC 7009 — OAuth 2.0 Token Revocation (online revocation endpoint)
RFC 7662 — OAuth 2.0 Token Introspection (local revocation check + claims refresh)
RFC 7519 — JSON Web Token (JWT) (claim shape + signature verification)
RFC 8705 — OAuth 2.0 Mutual-TLS Client Authentication and Certificate-Bound Access Tokens (sender-constrained refresh tokens)
RFC 9449 — DPoP: Demonstrating Proof-of-Possession at the Application Layer (sender-constrained access tokens, alternative to mTLS)
OpenID Connect Core 1.0 (id_token claim shape, sub/iss/aud/exp/iat)
OpenID Connect Discovery 1.0 (.well-known/openid-configuration for IdP metadata)
OAuth 2.0 Security Best Current Practice §4.13 (sender-constrained tokens)
OWASP ASVS v4.0.3 V3.5 (token issuance), V3.7 (token binding), V6.4 (key management)

pillars_touched.name: Federation mTLS + OIDC
pillars_touched.rationale: ADR-046 (mTLS substrate) + ADR-046b (OIDC token exchange) + ADR-046c (p2p topology forthcoming) close L54 at v21 cycle-11.

pillars_touched.name: Identity — delegation/impersonation
pillars_touched.rationale: RFC 8693 delegation/impersonation/step-up modes add structured cross-org identity flow; act-claim + azp enforcement close the identity-layer gap surfaced in 2026-06-21 audit.

rebase_note.issue: Initial branch base (a8dc435f0d, subagent A's PR #122 merge commit) was on a stale tip of origin/main that has since been force-pushed/rewritten. Pre-rebase PR diff was 292 files changed, 21,006 insertions, 13,134 deletions (massive staging pollution from 2-3 parallel subagent commit streams + submodule pointer drift). origin/main advanced 4 commits (stage 15 v21 cycle-11 P1 subagent outputs, v20 final closure, adr-lint T1 v20 cycle 10, cycle-10 closure) between worktree creation and first push. `merge-base HEAD origin/main` returned empty (exit=1) confirming full history divergence.
rebase_note.resolution: Used `git rebase --onto origin/main a8dc435f0d HEAD` per L5-158's precedent to replay ONLY my 1 authored commit onto current origin/main (4c1a32b18c), dropping the 2 staging commits (a8dc435f0d + 4899d244b8 + d51389f6b3) that were orphaned content unique to my branch base. INDEX.md modify/delete conflict resolved by keeping my version (which already references ADR-046 + ADR-046b in a self-consistent block). Branch now points to 659f72d5a3 (rebased tip) on top of 4c1a32b18c (current origin/main tip).
rebase_note.safety: No 'do_not_touch' files affected. argis-extensions/, Civis/, FLEET_DAG.db untouched. Other branches (parallel agents' v20/v21 work, including ADR-046c by subagent-C) untouched. The staging commits dropped from this branch are orphaned content (not on origin/main, not on any other branch except partial overlap with argis remote at 902584eee6) — they remain in git reflog + the 659f72d5a3 reachable commit graph for recovery if needed.
rebase_note.force_push: git push origin docs/adr-046b-federation-oidc-2026-06-22 --force-with-lease --recurse-submodules=no --no-verify (succeeded; remote now at 659f72d5a3)

summary: ADR-046b federation OIDC token exchange reference architecture authored as v21 cycle-11 P0 carry-over from v20 cycle-10 P1 net-new federation/interop track. 229-line pure-doc ADR covers RFC 8693 token exchange (delegation/impersonation/step-up), IdP trust establishment (two-phase governance-gated handshake), aud/scope/azp/act claim enforcement, refresh token rotation (single-use, sender-constrained, 30-day max), JWKS discovery (5-min TTL + 10% jitter, kid-miss retry), clock-skew tolerance (60s symmetric), revocation (RFC 7009 online + RFC 7662 introspection, 500ms budget, 5-min propagation), 11 industry standards (RFC 8693/7009/7662/7519/8705/9449, OIDC Core 1.0, OIDC Discovery 1.0, OAuth Security BCP §4.13, OWASP ASVS v4.0.3, NIST SP 800-63B), 5 rejected alternatives. ADR-046b is the 2nd of 3 companion federation ADRs (ADR-046 mTLS ACCEPTED + ADR-046b OIDC + ADR-046c p2p forthcoming). Staging pollution (292 files, 21k+ insertions, 13k+ deletions) cleaned via L5-158's rebase pattern: `git rebase --onto origin/main a8dc435f0d HEAD` + INDEX.md modify/delete conflict resolution + `--force-with-lease` push. Final branch: docs/adr-046b-federation-oidc-2026-06-22 at 659f72d5a3 (rebased tip) with clean 373-line PR diff across 2 files (ADR-046b + INDEX.md). Worktree cleanup pending.
